LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ADMIRAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 12, 2013 TO GIVE THE PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 9, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ARROWOOD SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. A R G U E D - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: ON THE SOLE ISSUE THAT THE PARTIES WERE PERMITTED TO RAISE BY WAY OF A CCP 437C(S)(2) MOTION, LEXINGTON HAD NO OBLIGATION TO DEFEND THE ADDITIONAL INSUREDS IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AFTER THEY WERE SUSPENDED BY THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD. HOWEVER, THAT DOES NOT FULLY ANSWER THE QUESTION OF WHETHER LEXINGTON HAS ANY OBLIGATION TO REIMBURSE ARROWOOD FOR SOME OF THE MONIES THAT ARROWOOD SPENT IN DEFENSE OF THE LEXINGTON'S ADDITIONAL INSUREDS IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AFTER THEY WERE SUSPENDED BY THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD. ON THAT ISSUE, WHICH IS REALLY WHAT THE PARTIES ARE SEEKING GUIDANCE, PER EQUITABLE PRINCIPLES, LEXINGTON IS OBLIGATED TO REIMBURSE ARROWOOD FOR A PORTION OF THE MONIES THAT ARROWOOD SPENT IN DEFENSE OF THE LEXINGTON'S ADDITIONAL INSUREDS IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AFTER THEY WERE SUSPENDED BY THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, BUT ONLY TO THE EXTENT THAT ARROWOOD'S DEFENSE OF THOSE PARTIES RESULTED IN A BENEFIT TO LEXINGTON BY REDUCING THE AMOUNT OF LEXINGTON'S INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS. THE PRECISE AMOUNT, IF ANY, OF SUCH REIMBURSEMENT CANNOT BE DETERMINED ON THIS MOTION BASED ON THE ABSENCE OF SUFFICIENT FACTS PRESENTED BY THE PARTIES. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiff Arrowood Surplus Lines Insurance Company
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JUAN A. TORRES, ESQ. of Musick, Peeler & Garrett LLP, Tel No. 213-629-7873 appearing for Plaintiff Arrowood Surplus Lines Insurance Company;
Atty. SCOTT LASALLE, ESQ. of the Aguilera Law Group, Tel No. 714-384-6600 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut;
Atty. MICHAEL HOUSHMAND, ESQ. and JENNIFER YU SACRO, ESQ. of Sacro & Walker LLP, Tel No. 818-721-9597 appearing for Defendant Lexington Insurance Co.;
Atty. LINDSEY A. MORGAN, ESQ. of Sinnott, Puebla, Campagne & Curet, Tel No. 415-352-6202 appearing for Defendant Steadfast Insurance Co.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiff Arrowood Surplus Lines Insurance Company, on the sole issue that the parties were permitted to raise by way of a CCP 437c(s)(2) motion, Lexington had no obligation to defend the Additional Insureds in the Underlying Action after they were suspended by the California Franchise Tax Board. However, that does not fully answer the question of whether Lexington has any obligation to reimburse Arrowood for some of the monies that Arrowood spent in defense of the Lexington's Additional Insureds in the Underlying Action after they were suspended by the California Franchise Tax Board. On that issue, which is really what the parties are seeking guidance, per equitable principles, Lexington is obligated to reimburse Arrowood for a portion of the monies that Arrowood spent in defense of the Lexington's Additional Insureds in the Underlying Action after they were suspended by the California Franchise Tax Board, but only to the extent that Arrowood's defense of those parties resulted in a benefit to Lexington by reducing the amount of Lexington's indemnity obligations. The precise amount, if any, of such reimbursement cannot be determined on this motion based on the absence of sufficient facts presented by the parties. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
